Q1 PASS verbatim. Q2 PASS. Q3 PASS ('4k' in quote). Q4 PASS. Q5 FAIL: compound_name in DB is '(4′-nitro-[1,1′-biphenyl]-4-yl)ethan-1-ol ( 4k )' which is truncated. The actual paper compound 4k is '1-(2′-bromo-[1,1′-biphenyl]-2-yl)-2-(4′-nitro-[1,1′-biphenyl]-4-yl)ethan-1-ol ( 4k )' — a two-biphenyl compound. The DB name omits the entire '1-(2′-bromo-[1,1′-biphenyl]-2-yl)-2-' fragment, making it represent a different (simpler) molecule. Q6 PASS. Q7 PASS.

Q5 FAIL (primary issue): compound_name is '(Sp)-bis(diphenylphosphinyl)[2.2]paracyclophane precursor (compound)' but the compound with M.p. 218–219°C is '(Sp)-2,2′-(1,4(1,4)-dibenzenacyclohexaphane-12,43-diyl)diphenol [(Sp)-2a]' — a diphenol compound, not a bis(diphenylphosphinyl) compound. The bis(diphenylphosphine oxide) compounds (Sp)-4a–4h have melting points in the 84–165°C range. Compound identity is wrong. Q6 PASS (DOI confirmed in PDF).

Q1 PASS verbatim. Q2 PASS. Q3 FAIL: no compound code or name in evidence_quote ('Yield 365 mg (91%); white solid; mp 63–64 o C,'). Q5 FAIL (primary issue): compound_name in DB is '(4S,13S)-N1,N14-di-Boc-tetradecane-1,4,13,14-tetraamine' but paper compound 5 is '(5R)-1,5-Di[(tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino]-dodecane' — a C12 diamine with 2 Boc groups (not C14 tetraamine). These are different molecules: different chain length (C12 vs C14), different number of amino groups (2 vs 4), different stereodescriptor (5R vs 4S,13S). Q4 PASS (mp value correct). Q6 PASS.
